human_verification.notes: Verified SaaS operations boundary handling, research-contract source coverage, execution-state controls, dependency evidence, content workflow awareness, confidentiality and approval controls, completion-evidence requirements, and scenario-to-ability mappings.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit SaaS operations execution, exception, refusal, confidentiality, tenant adaptation, and completion-evidence behavior.
Required abilities across shared, workflow-phase, and professional-and-information-services profile are fully covered and mapped.

specialist_owned_evidence: SaaS operations lifecycle progression is tied to named tenant, environment, service-order, ticket, runbook, change, monitoring, activation-test, QA, content, approval, and communication artifacts.
Blocked, held, stale, failed, partial, unsupported, owner-ambiguous, confidentiality-sensitive, and authority-sensitive work items are explicit exception cases.

Legal, editorial, quality signoff, delivery management, account, security, privacy, network engineering, software engineering, product, service-credit, billing, regulated outage, and contractual authority route to adjacent specialists.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; open questions are converted into explicit escalation, refusal, or tenant-fact dependencies.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
